response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his profile is a poor match for this role. He lists no Japanese proficiency (native-level is required), his recent decade of work is web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than C for UNIX, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel work, embedded switch firmware, or the required security technologies such as MACsec or IEEE 802.1X. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and general Linux familiarity do not offset the missing hard requirements.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and low-latency async I/O services in DLang
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and technical teaching experience
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, while native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
No demonstrated Linux kernel analysis/modification, embedded firmware, or L2/L3 switch experience
No background in the required security domain: MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, or encryption/key-exchange implementations

key_factors.factor: No Linux kernel or embedded networking/security experience
